citation_as_printed: G. A. Antcliffe, Phys. Rev. B 2, 345 (1970)
gap_observable: Kane two-band interaction gap from effective-mass nonparabolicity; source also reports photoconductive 50% threshold proxies
notes: Primary source recovered. x=0.204 with +/-0.003 spatial variation from relative spectral response and Cd-115 tracer analyses. Six Table I transport specimens and two photoconductive threshold states are reconstructed; exact Hansen low-temperature pairing remains unresolved.

citation_as_printed: C. T. Elliott, J. Melngailis, T. C. Harman, J. A. Kafalas, and W. C. Kernan, Phys. Rev. B 5, 2985 (1972)
gap_observable: Kane k.p signed zero-pressure gap inferred from pressure-dependent carrier concentrations
notes: Primary source recovered. Three named samples at x=0.149, 0.149, and 0.138 with electron-microprobe accuracy +/-0.005. Table I carrier summaries and five Table II model-derived gap candidates are reconstructed; the 77 K 7B value depends on assumed heavy-hole mass 0.3 or 0.7 m0, and exact Hansen marker mapping remains unresolved.

citation_as_printed: H. Kahlert and G. Bauer, Phys. Rev. Lett. 30, 1211 (1973)
gap_observable: temperature-dependent Kane nonparabolic-band gap inferred from magnetophonon resonance positions; source also reports SdH mass and g factor
notes: Primary source recovered. The source prints Eg(T)=0.09(1+0.0085T) eV over a 50-130 K magnetophonon experiment, with reported dEg/dT=7.6e-4 eV/K. Exact Hansen ingestion of the relation versus selected figure markers remains unresolved.

citation_as_printed: J. Stankiewicz and W. Giriat, Phys. Status Solidi 49, 387 (1972)
gap_observable: signed zero-pressure gap inferred from Hall coefficient using a simplified Kane/Groves-Paul carrier model
composition_method_stated_by_hansen: density measurements; homogeneity checked by Hall scan and electron microprobe
notes: Primary source recovered. Four composition-defined specimens at x=0.070, 0.095, 0.130, and 0.180 with +/-0.005 are reconstructed from Table 1 at 77 and 280 K, together with pressure/temperature coefficients and Equation 4. Exact Hansen HSC_R10 marker mapping remains unresolved.

citation_as_printed: S. H. Groves, T. C. Harman, and C. R. Pidgeon, Solid State Commun. 9, 451 (1971)
gap_observable: signed Gamma6-Gamma8 separation inferred from Pidgeon-Brown multiband Landau-level fitting; Gamma8 valence mass constrained by circular-polarization splitting
notes: Primary source recovered. One n-type (100) specimen is reconstructed with approximately twenty transitions, body-state gap estimates of -0.01 eV at 20-30 K and +0.01 eV at 90-100 K, and qualitative abstract signs at 4 and 77 K. Exact Hansen HSC_R11 marker mapping remains unresolved.

citation_as_printed: E. M. Aliev, S. A. Aliev, T. G. Gadzhiev, and M. I. Aliev, Phys. Status Solidi 40, K41 (1970)
gap_observable: conduction-band effective masses inferred from thermopower and Hall data; Table 1 signed gaps are adopted Wiley-Dexter Equation 3 inputs
notes: Primary source recovered. Eleven 300 K Table 1 mass records over x=0.05-0.20 are reconstructed. The five distinct signed-gap values are rounded evaluations of an adopted Wiley-Dexter relation and are not independent Aliev gap measurements; exact Hansen HSC_R12 marker mapping remains unresolved.
